title: Edward Gray
content:
Sweet Emma Moreland of yonder town
Met me walking on yonder way;
ï¿½And have you lost your heart?ï¿½ she said;
ï¿½And are you married yet, Edward Gray?ï¿½

Sweet Emma Moreland spoke to me;
Bitterly weeping I turnï¿½d away:
ï¿½Sweet Emma Moreland, love no more
Can touch the heart of Edward Gray.

ï¿½Ellen Adair she loved me well,
Against her fatherï¿½s and motherï¿½s will;
To-day I sat for an hour and wept
By Ellenï¿½s grave, on the windy hill.

ï¿½Shy she was, and I thought her cold,
Thought her proud, and fled over the sea;
Fillï¿½d I was with folly and spite,
When Ellen Adair was dying for me.

ï¿½Cruel, cruel the words I said!
Cruelly came they back to-day:
ï¿½Youï¿½re too slight and fickle,ï¿½ I said,
ï¿½To trouble the heart of Edward Gray.ï¿½

ï¿½There I put my face in the grassï¿½
Whisperï¿½d, ï¿½Listen to my despair;
I repent me of all I did;
Speak a little, Ellen Adair!ï¿½

ï¿½Then I took a pencil, and wrote
On the mossy stone, as I lay,
ï¿½Here lies the body of Ellen Adair;
And here the heart of Edward Gray!ï¿½

ï¿½Love may come, and love may go,
And fly, like a bird, from tree to tree;
But I will love no more, no more,
Till Ellen Adair come back to me.

ï¿½Bitterly wept I over the stone;
Bitterly weeping I turnï¿½d away.
There lies the body of Ellen Adair!
And there the heart of Edward Gray!ï¿½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Lord Tennyson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Alfred Lord Tennyson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
